metadata.llm_reasoning: Frontend calls each of the listed downstream services. A fault in any of these downstream services could propagate back to frontend, causing the symptom. Since the blast radius currently includes only frontend, the most plausible propagation path is root cause in one of the dependencies (e.g., cartservice, productcatalogservice, currencyservice, recommendationservice, checkoutservice, adservice, or shippingservice) affecting frontend performance or availability. Alternative paths would require knowledge of interactions between downstream services (not provided in topology), so based solely on given topology, no additional downstream-to-downstream propagation paths are evidenced. The topology supports the hypothesis that a downstream issue could explain the symptom, aligning with the log agent’s hint that there was no clear suspected downstream identified by logs alone. Within the blast radius, monitoring all dependencies remains prudent, with emphasis on services that are most central to user-facing operations (likely productcatalogservice and cartservice) due to their direct involvement in frontend functionality.

result.synthesis_reasoning: All sources agree the symptom is frontend-related and that a downstream dependency could be the root cause. Log Agent provides no observable errors, reducing concrete root-cause identification and elevating the plausibility of unobserved root causes within downstream dependencies. Topology confirms the dependencies and supports a plausible propagation path from downstream services to frontend. The TCB-RCA engine does not surface a concrete upstream/root cause beyond frontend in this dataset, reinforcing the need to consider unobserved root causes in downstream dependencies. Given the absence of observable errors and timing-based evidence, we cannot decisively pick a single root cause; thus we present a ranked set of plausible downstream candidates with equal likelihood and highlight currencyservice as the leading candidate due to its central role in the dependency set and the unobserved-root-cause pattern.
